textRun.content: Shared blueprint for LYFEOS + EntrepreneurOS (AgentOS) + CreatorOS
(separate repos, separate auth, separate DB, separate deployments — shared standards and reusable patterns)
This document defines the shared platform skeleton all three apps must follow so you can:
reuse architecture + code patterns across repos
avoid reinventing systems (agents, actions, notifications, etc.)
Each app remains its own SaaS product.
1) Repo & Folder Conventions (Same shape in each repo)
Each repo follows the same top-level layout:
components/ contains pure UI components (no fetching)
pages/ contains route views and orchestration
hooks/ contains data hooks (TanStack Query patterns)
styles/ contains theme tokens and global CSS
routes/ defines route modules by domain (tasks, agents, etc.)
services/ holds business logic (no express req/res inside)
integrations/ contains provider adapters (gmail, stripe, etc.)
middleware/ contains auth, rate limit, validation, error handling
shared/schema stores Drizzle schema (per-app; same naming standards)
shared/types contains DTOs and common types
shared/constants includes enums used on both server/client
2) Identity & Auth Standard (Separate per app, consistent behavior)
Each app uses its own auth, but must behave consistently:
All server APIs must support one consistent auth mode (choose per app):
session cookies (less preferred for your multi-app future)
Every app has its own users table, but the fields must align:
3) UI Design System Standard (Glass + Light/Dark + Consistent Components)
All apps must share a consistent UI language.
All repos must implement the same token names:
“glass” is applied to surfaces, NOT the whole page
3.4 Core component set (must exist in every repo)
Reusable components each app must include:
All POST/PATCH must validate with Zod
5) Events, Notifications, and Activity Logs (Shared Pattern)
Every app must implement an events table:
build activity UI using events table
6) Messaging Standard (Same architecture; different use cases)
Each app may implement messaging differently, but the schema pattern must match:
Even if each app uses different agents, they must share the same base interfaces.
All repos should expose one server module:
This is the most important shared engine across all three.
All actions in all apps use the same fields:
UI must render action cards showing:
validate payload (zod schema per action type)
Never store raw tokens in plaintext in DB without encryption.
store in server env for dev only
for prod: encrypted storage using KMS or at least app-level encryption
Search must be scoped (as you requested for CreatorOS) but the pattern is reusable.
search bar exists in top bar
search only applies to current module context
simple SQL ILIKE search in MVP
Each app defines KPIs, but component types stay the same:
Event-based analytics should use the events table.
No “save API keys” endpoints in production
Secrets stored in deployment platform secrets
All /api/* routes require auth unless explicitly public
13) Domain Module Mapping (What changes per app)
This is how you keep the skeleton identical while changing modules:
14) What this enables for you immediately
EntrepreneurOS can “donate” the agent/action engine pattern to the other repos
CreatorOS can “donate” marketplace/product patterns back later
LYFEOS can “donate” review loops + rituals UI patterns
If you want the next step, I’ll generate:
what to copy from EntrepreneurOS into LYFEOS and CreatorOS
Say: “generate transfer plan” and I’ll produce it.
Goal: reuse the same platform skeleton across EntrepreneurOS (AgentOS), LYFEOS, and CreatorOS while keeping separate repos / auth / DB / deployments.
This is a practical checklist: what to copy, what to refactor into portable kits, what NOT to copy, and the exact order to do it so you don’t create a tangled mess.
1) The Target: What “Core Kit” Actually Is
Even with separate repos, you want a shared internal standard consisting of 5 portable kits:
API Kit (client, DTOs, errors, pagination, validation patterns)
Auth Kit (middleware + client guards; app-specific providers configured per repo)
Agent Kit (provider abstraction + chat persistence + tool/action parsing)
Execution Kit (action approval schema + executor interface + audit logs)
Integrations Kit (OAuth + adapter interface + token storage patterns)
You won’t literally share one codebase right now; you’ll clone/sync these kits repo-to-repo with a clear boundary.
2) What to Copy From EntrepreneurOS First (Highest leverage)
EntrepreneurOS already has the strongest “OS engine” primitives. Start here.
A) UI Shell + Navigation Skeleton (copy pattern)
Layout shell component pattern (sidebar + header + main content)
Route guarding pattern from App.tsx (protected routes + auth pages)
EntrepreneurOS-specific nav labels, icons, route names
All three apps share identical “workspace” UX structure.
EntrepreneurOS already implements multi-provider AI logic.
Any endpoint that allows saving keys via API in production (dev-only)
LYFEOS and CreatorOS can immediately use the same AI interface.
C) Agent Chat + Memory Pattern (copy pattern)
EntrepreneurOS-specific agent roles/departments (those become per-app enums)
Same chat UX + storage works in all three apps.
D) Action Parsing + Action Records (copy code)
This is your moat: propose → approve → execute.
Status lifecycle (proposed → approved → queued → running → succeeded/failed)
Any direct “send email” implementation without a permission check layer
CreatorOS can use the exact same approval UI and executor pipeline for:
Gmail integration skeleton (as an example integration)
Any hardcoded environment assumptions from Replit
All apps connect integrations the same way:
Even if EntrepreneurOS has minimal event logging now, standardize it.
Same notification system across all apps.
3) What to Copy From LYFEOS Into EntrepreneurOS (to make EntrepreneurOS better)
LYFEOS will have “life OS mechanics” that make EntrepreneurOS more addictive and consistent.
Drift detection logic pattern (but for business KPIs)
Progress dashboards + streak logic (adapted to business rituals)
weekly content review (if you tie into CreatorOS later)
4) What to Copy From CreatorOS Into EntrepreneurOS (later phase)
course delivery (if EntrepreneurOS offers internal training)
But for MVP EntrepreneurOS: keep it focused.
CreatorOS feed tables should NOT go into EntrepreneurOS
LYFEOS habits tables should NOT go into CreatorOS
app-specific actions (unless they are in the action registry as a plugin)
any tight coupling between auth and app objects
6) The Action Catalog Standard (how each app stays different, cleanly)
Each app should define an Action Catalog that plugs into the same executor interface.
validate → preview → approve → execute → audit log
7) Transfer Execution Order (do this in this order)
Phase 1 — EntrepreneurOS → Core Kit (1–2 days of refactor)
Extract action parser + registry + executor interface into /kits/execution-kit
Deliverable: OS-Core-Kit v1 as a folder you can copy into other repos.
Phase 2 — Apply Core Kit into LYFEOS (fast)
Add agent chat using agent kit
Phase 3 — Apply Core Kit into CreatorOS (fast)
Add agent tools for content + DM automations
Add integrations skeleton (email provider, socials later)
Add action pipeline for scheduling + newsletters
Since you don’t want a monorepo, use one of these:
9) “Definition of Done” for v1 Core Kit
You are done when each repo has:
✅ same layout shell + responsive nav pattern
✅ security baseline (rate limits, no dev key endpoints, audit logs)
10) Immediate next output (if you want it)
If you want, I can write the exact Core Kit file list as a concrete blueprint, like:
…and I’ll map which existing EntrepreneurOS files correspond to each.
Just say: “list the core kit files” and I’ll generate the full file-level spec.

4. Do NOT hallucinate or infer beyond what is clearly supported.
